explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A press release states Thor Industries' explicit commitment to an AI strategy, which includes creating a new CIO role. This role is mandated to unlock enterprise-wide value from data and scale AI-enabled analytics. The company references a focus on operational responsibility, strategic asset management of data, and the disciplined execution of AI initiatives.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. It highlights Thor Industries' explicit commitment to an AI strategy by creating a new CIO role with a mandate to unlock enterprise-wide value from data and scale AI-enabled analytics. This demonstrates a focus on operational responsibility, strategic asset management of data, and the disciplined execution of AI initiatives.
relevant_sources.title: Thor Industries Elevates CIO Role for AI Strategy

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: THOR Industries' public disclosures include a press release stating an explicit commitment to an AI strategy. This commitment, alongside the description of a new CIO role with a mandate to unlock enterprise-wide value from data, represents the documented evidence for governance, which is the only pillar addressed out of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars, based on a review of 3 publicly available sources.
